readers.slice: deep-context + deep-research skills (SKILL.md, README.md, references/, scripts/, assets/)

findings.observation: deep-context is the INWARD codebase-context-gathering loop. Its deliverable is a reuse-first Context Report (REUSE catalog cited by file:symbol, integration points, touch list, conventions, pruned dependency subgraph, gaps), consumed by /speckit:plan and /speckit:implement. Distinguishing trait vs siblings: multi-model PARALLEL sweep over the SAME scope where cross-executor AGREEMENT drives confidence (default pool = 2 native @deep-context seats, optional heterogeneous CLI seats). Evidence: SKILL.md:10-14, README.md:30-40.
findings.merge_implication: In the merged deep-loop-workflows skill this becomes the 'context' mode/lane. Its unique persona is 'inward + agreement-weighted parallel sweep' — distinct from research's 'outward + sequential fresh-context-per-iteration'. The merged skill must preserve mode-specific vocabulary (reuse catalog, agreement, slice coverage) and not collapse it into research's novelty/sources vocabulary.

findings.observation: Single command surface: /deep:start-context-loop with two modes :auto (autonomous) and :confirm (gated at setup/iteration/synthesis). Executor pooling via --executor / --executors / --model / --concurrency flags (default native-only pool of 2; CLI seats opt-in). Loop type = 'context' in the deep-loop-runtime coverage-graph and convergence.cjs. Evidence: SKILL.md:412-426 (Quick Reference table), README.md:42-77.
findings.merge_implication: Modes (:auto/:confirm) and the executor-flag surface are IDENTICAL in shape to deep-research — generic scaffolding. The merged skill can share one mode/executor-flag contract across lanes; only the loop-type token ('context' vs 'research') and the default pool composition (2 native parallel vs 1 native sequential) differ.

findings.observation: Packet at {spec_folder}/context/: deep-context-config.json, deep-context-state.jsonl (append-only), deep-context-strategy.md, iterations/iteration-NNN.md, findings-registry.json (reducer-owned), deep-context-dashboard.md (reducer-owned), and the deliverable context-report.md + context-report.json. Standalone run dir only when no spec folder named/derivable (fail-closed). Evidence: SKILL.md:395-404; assets = context_report_template.md + deep_context_config.json.
findings.merge_implication: Artifact set is structurally parallel to deep-research's research/ packet (config/state.jsonl/strategy/dashboard/findings-registry/iterations are the SAME generic shape) but the deliverable name and template differ (context-report.md/.json + context_report_template.md vs research.md + no equivalent template). Merged skill needs per-lane artifact-naming config (context-* vs deep-research-*) and per-lane deliverable templates; the packet skeleton is shared scaffolding.

findings.observation: Five signals: 3 BLOCKING guards sliceCoverage(0.70)/agreementRate(0.50)/relevanceFloor(0.50) + 2 weighted contributors reuseCatalogCoverage(0.30 highest)/dependencyCompleteness(0.10). Stop score weights are reuse-first (0.30 reuse, 0.25 agreement, 0.20 slice, 0.15 relevance, 0.10 dependency). convergenceThreshold=0.10, agreementMin=2, relevanceGate=0.55, maxIterations=8. Critically: 'graph is the substrate the stop decision is computed from' — convergence runs ENTIRELY over the loop_type=context coverage graph (evaluateContext), unlike deep-research where the graph is an optional add-on. Evidence: SKILL.md:416-424; README.md:94-99; convergence_graph.md:16,24.
findings.merge_implication: deep-context convergence math is genuinely DIFFERENT from deep-research (agreement-rate guard + reuse-catalog weighting + mandatory coverage-graph substrate vs research's newInfoRatio). This is unique-per-lane domain logic, NOT generic scaffolding — the merged skill must carry per-lane signal definitions, thresholds, and weight tables. The coverage-graph dependency (loop_type='context' is mandatory not optional) is a hard backend coupling to preserve.

findings.area: deep-context: owns vs calls into deep-loop-runtime
findings.observation: OWNS uniquely: agreement-weighted reducer scripts/reduce-state.cjs (801 lines), the Context Report synthesis + template, the by-model parallel-sweep dispatch pattern, the 5 context-specific convergence signals, and notably scripts/loop-lock.cjs (114 lines) lives LOCALLY in deep-context. CALLS INTO deep-loop-runtime: coverage-graph (coverage-graph-db.ts VALID_KINDS.context/CONTEXT_WEIGHTS), convergence.cjs --loop-type context, multi-seat parallel dispatch, executor config, and the durability layer (writeStateAtomic, repairJsonlTail, post-dispatch-validate, recursion-guard). Evidence: SKILL.md:301,388-393; convergence_graph.md:36-38; Glob shows loop-lock.cjs in deep-context/scripts.
findings.merge_implication: deep-context has a LOCAL loop-lock.cjs copy whereas deep-research references the runtime's loop-lock — a divergence the merge must reconcile (one canonical lock, likely in deep-loop-runtime). The agreement reducer and report synthesis are lane-unique and must move into deep-loop-workflows. The runtime coupling (convergence.cjs, coverage-graph, durability) is already correctly externalized to deep-loop-runtime and should stay there.

findings.observation: deep-research is the OUTWARD/web knowledge-discovery loop: iterative investigation with FRESH CONTEXT PER ITERATION, externalized state, convergence by newInfoRatio. Each iteration dispatches a fresh @deep-research LEAF agent that investigates ONE focus, writes iteration-NNN.md + JSONL delta, returns. Deliverable = research/research.md. Adapted from karpathy/autoresearch, AGR Ralph Loop, pi-autoresearch. Evidence: SKILL.md:11-12,347-351; README.md:30-41.
findings.merge_implication: This is the 'research' mode/lane. Its unique persona is sequential single-focus fresh-context iteration with novelty-based stopping — orthogonal to context's parallel agreement sweep. The merged skill must keep research's distinct vocabulary (novelty, sources, negative knowledge, question coverage) per SKILL.md:439.

findings.observation: Command /deep:start-research-loop with :auto/:confirm. But deep-research has RICHER lifecycle than deep-context: explicit lifecycle modes new/resume/restart (runtime-supported) plus deferred fork/completed-continue; typed JSONL events (resumed/restarted); flat-first vs pt-NN packet allocation logic; multi-lineage fan-out via --executor/--executors (each lineage an independent full loop in lineages/{label}/). Iteration status enum complete|timeout|error|stuck|insight|thought. Evidence: SKILL.md:293-302,336-343,404-417.
findings.merge_implication: deep-research carries substantial UNIQUE lifecycle machinery (resume/restart generations, flat-first/pt-NN packet allocation, lineage fan-out, insight/thought statuses) that deep-context's SKILL.md does NOT document. The merge cannot assume a single uniform lifecycle — either research's richer lifecycle becomes the shared baseline (and context adopts it) or per-lane lifecycle config is needed. This is the single largest behavioral asymmetry in the slice.

findings.observation: convergenceThreshold default 0.05 on newInfoRatio (fully-new=1.0, partial=0.5, +0.10 simplicity bonus, capped 1.0). Quality gates: source diversity, focus alignment, no single-weak-source. Stuck-recovery after stuck threshold (default 3). SKILL.md explicitly warns thresholds are 'NOT INTERCHANGEABLE with siblings' (deep-review 0.10 on severity, deep-ai-council 0.20 on verdict stability). Evidence: SKILL.md:27-37,85-87(README).
findings.merge_implication: deep-research's SKILL.md is the ONLY one in the slice that explicitly documents the cross-sibling threshold-non-portability invariant. The merged skill MUST encode per-lane convergence defaults as a guarded table and preserve a warning that thresholds do not carry across lanes — collapsing to one threshold would silently break iteration counts. This is a known landmine the spec must call out.

findings.observation: deep-research uniquely OWNS a bounded spec.md mutation contract (spec_check_protocol.md, 231 lines): late-INIT advisory lock at research/.deep-research.lock, folder_state classification (no-spec/spec-present/spec-just-created-by-this-run/conflict-detected), pre-init seeding, and post-synthesis write-back into exactly one BEGIN/END GENERATED fence. Also resource-map.md promotion to canonical packet state. deep-context has NO equivalent — it explicitly must NOT mutate spec.md (that is /speckit:plan's job, SKILL.md:308). Evidence: deep-research SKILL.md:331-363; spec_check_protocol.md:14-30; deep-context SKILL.md:308.
findings.merge_implication: spec.md write-back + resource-map promotion are research-ONLY behaviors with a dedicated 231-line protocol and a unique advisory lock. The merge must scope these as lane-specific capabilities (only the research lane writes spec.md fences); a uniform 'all lanes write spec.md' or 'no lane writes spec.md' rule would break one side. This is unique domain logic, not scaffolding.

findings.area: deep-research: owns vs calls into deep-loop-runtime
findings.observation: OWNS uniquely: reduce-state.cjs (1072 lines — larger than context's 801; also acts as convergence EVALUATOR per README:199), runtime-capabilities.cjs (114 lines), spec_check_protocol, the research synthesis, prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl, runtime_capabilities.json + capability_matrix.md (runtime parity), convergence_reference_only.md (deferred design), and verify-yaml-script-paths.sh. CALLS INTO deep-loop-runtime: shared executor/state/coverage-graph runtime, fanout-run.cjs (lineage dispatch). Notably deep-research does NOT carry a local loop-lock.cjs (uses runtime's). Evidence: deep-research SKILL.md:497-499; README.md:199-205; Glob inventory.
findings.merge_implication: deep-research carries 2 scripts (reduce-state 1072L + runtime-capabilities 114L) + a YAML-path verifier .sh, vs deep-context's 2 (reduce-state 801L + loop-lock 114L). The two reduce-state.cjs files are SEPARATE implementations with different convergence logic and sizes — they cannot be naively unified; the merge needs either a shared reducer core with lane plugins or two retained reducers. runtime-capabilities.cjs + capability_matrix + runtime_capabilities.json are a research-only parity subsystem.

findings.observation: BOTH skills use the identical subfolder family structure: references/{guides,protocol,convergence,state}/ plus assets/. Shared filenames present in both: guides/quick_reference.md, protocol/loop_protocol.md, convergence/{convergence,convergence_signals,convergence_recovery,convergence_graph}.md, state/{state_format,state_jsonl,state_outputs,state_reducer_registry}.md. The smart-router pseudocode, INTENT_SIGNALS/RESOURCE_MAP shape, _guard_in_skill guard, discover_markdown_resources, and ALWAYS/CONDITIONAL/ON_DEMAND loading levels are near-identical generic scaffolding (both cite the sk-doc smart-router template). Evidence: deep-context SKILL.md:95-233; deep-research SKILL.md:117-266; both Glob inventories.
findings.merge_implication: The reference taxonomy (guides/protocol/convergence/state), the smart-router engine, and the loading-level machinery are GENERIC SCAFFOLDING duplicated across both skills (and per the SKILL.md prose, across all siblings). The merge can collapse these into ONE shared router + ONE reference taxonomy with lane-namespaced content files, eliminating significant duplication. This is the highest-leverage consolidation in the slice.

findings.observation: deep-context references: 10 files, ~1599 lines total (largest: state_jsonl.md 313, quick_reference.md 217). deep-research references: 13 files, ~2552 lines total (largest: loop_protocol.md 616, quick_reference.md 234, spec_check_protocol.md 231, state_jsonl.md 238). deep-research has 3 EXTRA reference files with no context equivalent: protocol/spec_check_protocol.md, convergence/convergence_reference_only.md, guides/capability_matrix.md. deep-research's loop_protocol.md (616L) is ~5x larger than context's (116L). Evidence: Grep line-count outputs.
findings.merge_implication: deep-research's reference corpus is ~60% larger and structurally richer (extra spec-check, reference-only, capability-matrix files; far larger loop_protocol). A naive 'pick one set' merge would lose research-specific depth. The spec should treat reference content as per-lane payload under a shared taxonomy: shared files (quick_reference, state_*, convergence core) get lane sections; lane-unique files (spec_check_protocol, capability_matrix, convergence_reference_only, convergence_graph-as-mandatory-substrate) are carried as lane-scoped.

findings.observation: BOTH skills ship feature_catalog/ and manual_testing_playbook/ directories (referenced in both READMEs §8 and SKILL refs, e.g. deep-context convergence_graph.md:38 points into feature_catalog/06--coverage-graph-schema/). These were NOT in the references/scripts/assets glob (separate top-level dirs) but are load-bearing per-skill validation packages with category structure. Evidence: deep-context README.md:174-202; deep-research README.md:166-207; convergence_graph.md:38.
findings.merge_implication: Each skill has its own feature_catalog + manual_testing_playbook with skill-specific categories (context: frontier-seeding/sweep/agreement/coverage-graph-schema; research: loop-lifecycle/state/convergence/research-output). The merge must consolidate or namespace these per-lane; the playbook's hand-maintained file-count self-checks (a known drift hazard) will need updating. NOTE: these dirs were outside the assigned glob scope — flagged as a gap to map in a follow-up slice.

findings.observation: deep-context documents a 3-runtime native-seat mirror requirement: .opencode/agents/deep-context.md (canonical) + .claude/agents/deep-context.md + .codex/agents/deep-context.toml, identical bodies, differing frontmatter; a missing mirror = silent native-seat dispatch failure. deep-research SKILL.md:17-21 documents the same runtime path resolution (.opencode/.claude/.codex agents) but does not restate the full mirror-sync table. Evidence: deep-context SKILL.md:277-287,302; deep-research SKILL.md:17-21.
findings.merge_implication: Both lanes dispatch a by-name native seat resolved per-runtime, requiring 3 mirror files EACH (@deep-context and @deep-research agents). The merge to one skill does NOT automatically merge the agent seats — the spec must decide whether deep-loop-workflows keeps per-mode agent names (@deep-context/@deep-research) or a unified @deep-loop agent with mode param. Either way the 3-runtime mirror-sync discipline (canonical + .claude + .codex) carries forward as a migration hazard.

findings.observation: Frontmatter differs: deep-context allowed-tools=[Read,Write,Edit,Bash,Glob,Grep] (no Task/WebFetch/memory_*), version 1.2.0. deep-research allowed-tools=[Read,Write,Edit,Bash,Grep,Glob,Task,WebFetch,memory_context,memory_search], version 1.13.1.0, plus an argument-hint field. deep-research allows Task (for the command executor) + WebFetch + memory tools because it is outward/web; deep-context omits them because it is inward/read-only-analyzer. Evidence: deep-context SKILL.md:1-6; deep-research SKILL.md:1-7.
findings.merge_implication: The merged skill's allowed-tools must be the UNION (Task, WebFetch, memory_* needed by research lane; the context lane is stricter read-only). Version numbers are wildly divergent (1.2.0 vs 1.13.1.0) — the merge needs a fresh unified version baseline. The tool-permission asymmetry (web/memory only for research) is a real per-lane constraint, not just metadata.

open_questions: feature_catalog/ and manual_testing_playbook/ directories exist for both skills but were outside the assigned references/scripts/assets glob — their full category structure, file counts, and the hand-maintained file-count self-checks need a dedicated mapping slice before the migration spec quantifies validation-package consolidation effort.

The two reduce-state.cjs files (deep-context 801L vs deep-research 1072L) were line-counted but not diffed at the function level — confirming how much logic is genuinely shared vs lane-specific (and whether a shared reducer core is extractable) requires reading both script bodies.

deep-context carries a LOCAL scripts/loop-lock.cjs (114L) while deep-research references deep-loop-runtime's loop-lock — need to confirm whether deep-context's local copy is a divergent fork or an identical mirror of the runtime lock before deciding the canonical home in the merge.

deep-research SKILL.md references scripts/reduce-state.cjs + scripts/runtime-capabilities.cjs but the assets glob also surfaced verify-yaml-script-paths.sh (a YAML-path verifier) not mentioned in either README §8 scripts list — its role and whether it is migration-relevant is unconfirmed.

readers.slice: deep-review + deep-ai-council skills (current-state map for merge into deep-loop-workflows)

findings.observation: deep-review is an autonomous iterative CODE-REVIEW loop (one dimension per iteration, fresh context per pass, externalized state). Four review dimensions live in assets/review_mode_contract.yaml: Correctness, Security, Spec-Alignment/Traceability, Completeness/Maintainability (correctness+security required for coverage). It is invoked EXCLUSIVELY via /deep:start-review-loop (:auto | :confirm). The @deep-review LEAF agent is single-iteration and command-driven; no direct Task dispatch. allowed-tools includes Task (for the command executor) but NOT WebFetch (code-only, no web).
findings.merge_implication: In the merged skill this becomes the 'review' mode/lane. Its no-web constraint is the inverse of deep-research (which has web) — the merged skill must keep mode-scoped tool allowances, not a single union allowed-tools. SKILL.md version is 1.10.2.0 (highest of the two), the per-mode invocation surface is a distinct command (/deep:start-review-loop).

findings.observation: Severity is P0 (weight 10, blocks PASS) / P1 (weight 5, CONDITIONAL) / P2 (weight 1, PASS advisory). Loop ends in exactly one verdict PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL with a VERDICT_LOCK rule (any confirmed active P0 forces FAIL). Every iteration-NNN.md MUST end with the exact final line 'Review verdict: PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL' (parsed by exact string match by synthesis + CI gate). Final report review-report.md carries 9 core sections plus a conditional Resource Map Coverage Gate section. State packet under {spec_folder}/review/ (flat-first, pt-NN only on collision).
findings.merge_implication: The P0/P1/P2 + verdict-lock + exact-final-line contract is deep-review's unique-owned output contract — it has NO analogue in council (which uses two-of-three convergence + non-converged). The merged skill must preserve review's verdict grammar and the iteration final-line string match verbatim; a generic 'finding' schema cannot absorb it without breaking the CI gate parser.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:333-377 (severity table, verdicts, verdict lock, final-line contract), :440-476 (success criteria, 9 sections), :300-308 (state packet location)

findings.observation: Convergence is a three-signal weighted vote: Rolling Average (0.30), MAD Noise Floor (0.25), Dimension Coverage (0.45); composite stop threshold default 0.60. Nine legal-stop gates must all pass before STOP is legal (finding stability, dimension coverage, P0 resolution, evidence density, hotspot saturation, claim adjudication, fix-completeness replay, candidate coverage, graphless fallback). P0 override forces another iteration on any fresh P0. Default convergence threshold cited as deep-review 0.10 vs deep-research 0.05 vs deep-ai-council 0.20 (sibling-parity note).
findings.merge_implication: Each sibling has its OWN convergence weights, threshold, and gate bundle — they are NOT interchangeable. The merged skill needs per-mode convergence config (review's 9-gate bundle + 0.60 composite vs council's two-of-three). Convergence reference docs are duplicated per skill (deep-review/references/convergence/*, deep-ai-council/references/convergence/*) and will need de-duplication or mode-namespacing.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:93-97 (three-signal vote, 9 gates), SKILL.md:21 (sibling-parity threshold note), references/convergence/convergence.md (per skill)

findings.observation: deep-review's in-skill reducer scripts/reduce-state.cjs requires ONLY system-spec-kit shared helpers (resource-map/extract-from-evidence.cjs, shared/review-research-paths.cjs) — NO direct deep-loop-runtime require. Runtime consumption happens at the WORKFLOW/reference layer: loop_protocol.md cites deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts (parseExecutorConfig) for executor resolution, and state_format.md cites deep-loop-runtime/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts for coverage evidence. runtime-capabilities.cjs has no runtime require. Unique-owned scripts: reduce-state.cjs, runtime-capabilities.cjs (2 scripts).
findings.merge_implication: deep-review is LESS coupled to the runtime than council — its reducer is self-contained and the runtime touch is config/coverage at the YAML layer. Migration of review is mostly a relocation (SKILL.md + references + 2 scripts + assets) with the YAML command rewiring; the reducer moves cleanly. The runtime boundary (executor-config + coverage-graph) is already external and stable.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:11-14 (requires only system-spec-kit), references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:276,754 (executor-config.ts), references/state/state_format.md:415 (coverage-graph-db.ts), runtime-capabilities.cjs (no runtime require)

findings.observation: deep-ai-council is a PLANNING-ONLY multi-seat deliberation skill (2-3 seats, six reasoning lenses: Analytical/Creative/Critical/Pragmatic/Holistic/Research). Two operating surfaces: (1) single-pass @ai-council LEAF agent, (2) DEEP MODE iterative multi-topic via /deep:ask-ai-council (:auto|:confirm), capped 3 rounds/topic + 5 topics/session. One-CLI-per-round invariant: all seats in a round use the SAME CLI; cross-CLI is staged as separate rounds. Primarily in-CLI (uses the runtime's own model bench); external cli-* dispatch is secondary. allowed-tools: Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Glob/Grep (NO Task — it is a planning LEAF). version 2.2.0.0.
findings.merge_implication: Council is the 'council/planning' mode/lane. It is structurally the MOST different sibling: two invocation surfaces (agent + deep-mode command), session>topic>round hierarchy (no single 'iteration' unit), Write/Edit ALLOWED but scoped to ai-council/** only. The merged skill must keep council's write-scope guard and the one-CLI-per-round invariant as mode-specific rules; they do not generalize to review (read-only) or context (read-only).
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/deep-ai-council/SKILL.md:1-6 (frontmatter, version 2.2.0.0), :18-31 (in-CLI primary modes), :337-377 (rules incl one-CLI-per-round), README.md:58-68 (deep mode caps), :99-113 (six lenses, three critique roles)

findings.observation: Artifacts persist under packet-local ai-council/** tree: append-only ai-council-state.jsonl (every event, ending with required council_complete event), per-seat outputs seats/round-NNN/, deliberation + critique notes, council-report.md, and failed rounds moved to failed/round-NNN-<timestamp>/ for forensics. Persistence is caller-owned via scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs (fail-closed if required output_schema section missing); completion verified by scripts/advise-council-completion.cjs. Council graph support is a DERIVED PROJECTION rebuilt from artifacts (never source of truth).
findings.merge_implication: Council's artifact contract (ai-council/** + council_complete event + failed/ forensics + derived graph) is entirely distinct from review's {spec_folder}/review/ packet and P0/P1/P2 registry. The merged skill cannot share one artifact schema across modes — it needs per-mode artifact roots and writers. persist-artifacts.cjs / advise-council-completion.cjs are council-unique scripts that must migrate intact.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/deep-ai-council/SKILL.md:341-355 (ai-council/** scope, council_complete, derived-projection rule), README.md:71-86 (artifact tree, persist + verify), :144-154 (failed/ forensics)

findings.observation: Convergence is the TWO-OF-THREE rule: two of three seats agree on the material plan AND the critique pass (Hunter/Skeptic/Referee) finds no new high-severity blocker; otherwise run completes as 'non-converged' (an honest stop, not fabricated consensus). Adjudication = three critique roles: Hunter attacks, Skeptic defends/separates real flaws from trade-offs, Referee adjusts scores and records adjustments (five-dimension scoring rubric in references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md). Default convergence threshold cited as 0.20.
findings.merge_implication: Council's two-of-three + Hunter/Skeptic/Referee adjudicator is a third, distinct convergence/adjudication model (review uses the same H/S/R names for P0 ADVERSARIAL self-check, but council uses them for cross-SEAT critique scoring — same vocabulary, different mechanism). The merged skill must NOT collapse these: H/S/R means different things in review vs council. Flag this naming collision explicitly in the migration spec.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/deep-ai-council/README.md:104-111 (two-of-three, H/S/R critique), SKILL.md:14 (depth dispatch), references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md; cf. deep-review/README.md:99-101 (H/S/R as P0 self-check)

findings.observation: deep-ai-council DIRECTLY requires the runtime's lib/council primitives from inside its own scripts: orchestrate-topic.cjs and orchestrate-session.cjs import multi-seat-dispatch.cjs, round-state-jsonl.cjs, adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs, cost-guards.cjs, session-state-hierarchy.cjs from ../../deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/. replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs shells out to deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --loop-type council for the derived graph. The runtime ships 9 lib/council files (5 .cjs primitives consumed + convergence.cjs + council-graph-db.ts + council-graph-query.ts + README). Council-unique in-skill scripts: persist-artifacts.cjs, advise-council-completion.cjs, orchestrate-topic.cjs, orchestrate-session.cjs, replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs, lib/ (findings-registry, rollback, audit-trail, persist-artifacts).
findings.merge_implication: Council is ALREADY partially migrated to the two-skill architecture: its deliberation/convergence/cost-guard/state primitives live in deep-loop-runtime/lib/council and the skill orchestrates them. This is the reference template for the merge — the merged deep-loop-workflows skill should consume runtime primitives the same way for all modes. The relative require path ../../deep-loop-runtime stays valid if the merged skill sits at the same .opencode/skills/ depth; if nesting changes, every require/import path (and the test imports using ../../../) must be re-pathed.

findings.observation: deep-review references shape: protocol/ (quick_reference, loop_protocol, loop_state_and_gates), convergence/ (convergence, convergence_signals, convergence_recovery), state/ (state_format, state_jsonl, state_outputs, state_reducer_registry) = 10 ref docs. Scripts: reduce-state.cjs, runtime-capabilities.cjs + tests/fixtures (2 prod scripts). Assets: review_mode_contract.yaml, deep_review_config.json/strategy.md/dashboard.md, prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl, runtime_capabilities.json. deep-ai-council references shape: integration/ (quick_reference, loop_protocol, graph_support), convergence/ (convergence_signals, deep_mode, depth_dispatch, failure_handling), structure/ (state_format, folder_layout, output_schema), scoring/ (scoring_rubric, findings_registry), patterns/ (seat_diversity_patterns, command_wiring, anti_patterns) = 15 ref docs. Scripts: 5 prod + lib/ (4 modules) + node_modules + extensive vitest. Assets: deep_ai_council_config.json/strategy.md/dashboard.md, prompt_pack_round.md, runtime_capabilities.json.
findings.merge_implication: Both skills use a SAME-NAMED but differently-organized reference taxonomy: deep-review groups protocol/convergence/state; council groups integration/convergence/structure/scoring/patterns. There is naming overlap (both have convergence/convergence_signals.md, both have state_format.md, both have loop_protocol.md/quick_reference.md, both have a strategy.md + dashboard.md + config.json + runtime_capabilities.json asset). The merge MUST mode-namespace these (e.g. references/review/, references/council/) or content will collide on filename. Both ship a runtime_capabilities.json + a per-skill capabilities script — candidate for consolidation into the runtime.

findings.observation: Both SKILL.md/README explicitly position themselves as siblings sharing deep-loop-runtime alongside deep-research and deep-context, with per-skill 'Sibling Deep Loops' tables that cross-reference all four other loops by name. Both reference convergence threshold parity (deep-review 0.10 / deep-research 0.05 / deep-ai-council 0.20). Both have feature_catalog/ + manual_testing_playbook/ validation packages (council: 32 scenarios / 9 categories; review: loop lifecycle/state/convergence/output categories).
findings.merge_implication: The cross-sibling reference tables and threshold-parity notes are scattered across every skill's docs — after the merge these five sibling references collapse into intra-skill mode references, so EVERY 'Sibling Deep Loops' table and the convergence-parity note become stale and must be rewritten as mode comparisons. feature_catalog + manual_testing_playbook packages (per skill) also need consolidation/mode-namespacing. These are doc-surface migration tasks, distinct from the code/script migration.

readers.slice: deep-improvement skill (Lane A agent-improvement, Lane B model-benchmark + sweep, Lane C skill-benchmark, Lane D non-dev-ai-system)

findings.observation: deep-improvement carries FOUR co-equal lanes in ONE skill, not one mode with variations. SKILL.md §1 table and README §4 both enumerate: Lane A agent-improvement (/deep:start-agent-improvement-loop), Lane B model-benchmark (/deep:start-model-benchmark-loop), Lane C skill-benchmark (/deep:start-skill-benchmark-loop), Lane D non-dev-ai-system-refine (/deep:start-non-dev-ai-system-loop). Each lane has its own references/, assets/, and scripts/ subtree (agent_improvement / model_benchmark / skill_benchmark / non_dev_ai_system) plus a shared/ tree. loop-host.cjs VALID_MODES set = {agent-improvement, model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, non-dev-ai-system-refine} confirms 4 runtime modes wired.
findings.merge_implication: This is the single biggest reason deep-improvement is the hardest merge target. The merged deep-loop-workflows skill must preserve four distinct command entry points and four distinct resource subtrees. A naive 'one mode per skill' flattening breaks here: deep-improvement is already a four-mode skill internally. The merge must treat each lane as a peer mode alongside the other four skills' modes, not collapse them.

findings.observation: Lane A scores a bounded agent .md via deterministic 5-dimension scoring (dynamic mode is the ONLY path; no static profiles ship). Dimensions + weights: Structural Integrity 0.20, Rule Coherence 0.25, Integration Consistency 0.25, Output Quality 0.15, System Fitness 0.15. Profiles generated on-the-fly by generate-profile.cjs; scored by score-candidate.cjs. Every check is regex/string/file-existence (no model-as-judge). Output artifacts land under {spec_folder}/improvement/: candidates/, benchmark-outputs/, agent-improvement-state.jsonl, improvement-journal.jsonl, experiment-registry.json, agent-improvement-dashboard.md.
findings.merge_implication: Lane A's scorer/profiler/journal/reducer seam is the deepest of the four. The merged skill must keep score-candidate.cjs + generate-profile.cjs + the 5-dim weights table intact and lane-scoped. Its packet-local artifact namespace (agent-improvement-*) is distinct from Lane B/C/D outputs and must not collide post-merge.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md:277-287 (5-dim table + dynamic-only); README.md:82-94; references/shared/quick_reference.md:69-92 (weights + runtime layout)

findings.observation: Lane B benchmarks a model/prompt framework (not an agent file) via loop-host --mode=model-benchmark, reusing 3 pluggable seams (candidate-source, dispatcher, scorer). Scorer selection: --scorer pattern (default heading matcher) OR --scorer 5dim (ported 120/003 five-dim scorer score-model-variant.cjs); grader noop|mock|llm. SEPARATE from the agent-improvement 5-dim scorer. Lane B ALSO has a distinct sweep-benchmark.cjs: a config-driven cartesian matrix expander that runs framework-bakeoff / model-vs-model profiles, with correctness as a GATE (correctness-gate.cjs / applyGate) producing a WINNER/TIE/INCONCLUSIVE trust verdict (DEFAULT_MIN_SAMPLES_FOR_WINNER=3, insufficient_n -> INCONCLUSIVE). Outputs always write to the sk-prompt-small-model hub at .opencode/skills/sk-prompt-small-model/benchmarks/{run_label}/ (NO spec-local path).
findings.merge_implication: Lane B is internally TWO sub-capabilities (single run-benchmark vs sweep-benchmark matrix), each with its own scorer plumbing and verdict logic, plus a cross-skill output dependency on sk-prompt-small-model's benchmark tree. The merge cannot localize Lane B output; it writes into a sibling skill's tree. This external write target and the correctness-gate verdict subsystem must be carried verbatim.

findings.observation: Lane C diagnoses a SKILL (routing/discovery/efficiency/usefulness), diagnostic-only (does NOT mutate the target). Scoring contract is D1-D5 with point weights D1=25(inter12+intra13), D2=20, D3=15, D4=25, D5=15(hard gate). Two scoring modes: Mode A router-replay (deterministic, default) and Mode B live trace. D5 is a static hard gate run first (P0/P1/P2 penalties; any P0 = gateFailed). D4 (hallucination grader) is intentionally UNSCORED in the aggregate; real usefulness is the opt-in D4-R task-outcome ablation surfaced as an advisory signal, never folded in. D1-inter uses the deterministic SQLite advisor probe (--advisor-mode=python, opt-in). Orchestrator run-skill-benchmark.cjs; many sub-scripts (score-skill-benchmark, d4-ablation, d5-connectivity, build-report, advisor-probe, contamination-lint, browser-executor, playbook-generator). Output: ranked remediable Skill Benchmark Report mapped through remediation_taxonomy.json.
findings.merge_implication: Lane C has the most distinct scorer (D1-D5 weighted with normalize-over-measured, advisory-signal carve-outs, hard gate, advisor-probe dependency, contamination linter) and the largest sub-script fan-out of any lane. It is also the only lane whose target is itself a skill, creating a self-referential risk once all deep loops live in deep-loop-workflows. The merge must keep the entire skill_benchmark scoring contract and its remediation taxonomy as a self-contained module; its advisor-probe couples it to the skill-advisor subsystem.

findings.observation: Lane D benchmarks an AI-system PACKAGING and auto-refines its technique docs behind hard guardrails. Critically, the guarded loop host is PACKAGING-OWNED (<packaging-root>/benchmark/_loop/loop.py + _gates/gates.py + _gates/derive.py), NOT in deep-improvement; loop-host only adapts via run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs which spawns python3. dry-run is DEFAULT (--run for live). Independent different-family grader required (self-grades inflate ~+6/25; grader-family violation is a HARD kill-switch). Kill-switches HALT without promoting (scoring-surface drift, derived-copy drift, grader-family violation, hard-blocker lint, concurrent-run lock). Session ends at FIRST accepted promotion (promotedStop) into an isolated worktree; at most one promotion per run. Onboarding via assets/non_dev_ai_system/templates/*.template rendered by init_packaging.py.
findings.merge_implication: Lane D is architecturally unique: its core loop lives OUTSIDE the skill (in the packaging under test), so the merge inherits only a thin adapter + contract docs + templates, not the loop itself. Its Python boundary, kill-switch taxonomy, and packaging-owned freeze/derive surfaces are guardrails that must survive the merge unchanged. This is the lane least entangled with shared deep-loop-runtime state but most entangled with an external file contract.

findings.area: Shared seam vs lane-specific seam split
findings.observation: The four lanes deliberately SHARE three pluggable seams: candidate-source, dispatcher, scorer, plus a shared scripts/shared/ tree (loop-host.cjs router, reduce-state.cjs reducer, promote-candidate.cjs, improvement-journal.cjs, mutation-coverage.cjs, materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs). Lane A keeps the default agent-improvement path BYTE-IDENTICAL when no --mode flag is set. The reducer carries per-record mode metadata (mode: agent-improvement|model-benchmark) so results attribute per lane. promote-candidate.cjs is single-canonical-guard shared by Lane A (agent-scored gate) and Lane B (benchmark-report path) only.
findings.merge_implication: deep-improvement already implements the exact pattern the merge wants (one host, multiple modes, shared seams, mode-tagged state). This is the strongest merge ASSET: the loop-host.cjs mode-router + shared reducer is a ready template for how deep-loop-workflows could dispatch all five skills' modes. But it also means the merge must NOT break the byte-identical default-path guarantee or the mode-tagged record contract the reducer depends on.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md:300-305 (seams, byte-identical, mode metadata); scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs:9-29,147-156 (mode->script routing); SKILL.md:479-507 (reducer consumer)

findings.observation: SKILL.md §2 ships a ~120-line Python smart-router with 10 weighted intents (QUICK_REFERENCE, LOOP_EXECUTION, EVALUATION_POLICY, PROMOTION_OPERATIONS, TARGET_ONBOARDING, INTEGRATION_SCAN, MODEL_BENCHMARK w5, SKILL_BENCHMARK w5, NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM w5, FULL_SETUP), a RESOURCE_MAP, and a RUNTIME_ASSETS map all keyed by lane. Resources are organized by lane folder; the router loads only the matching lane's references/assets.
findings.merge_implication: The router is itself lane-partitioned and intent-scored. Merging five skills means the merged router must absorb deep-improvement's 10 intents PLUS the other four skills' intents without keyword collision (e.g. generic 'benchmark' already routes here). The lane-keyed RESOURCE_MAP/RUNTIME_ASSETS pattern is reusable but the intent keyword space will need de-confliction across all five merged skills.

findings.observation: The skill is the largest of the five by file count: separate feature_catalog/ (six categories: 01-evaluation-loop, 02-integration-scanning, 03-scoring-system, 04-model-benchmark-mode, 05-skill-benchmark, 06-non-dev-ai-system, ~24 feature files + index) and a manual_testing_playbook/ tree, plus a changelog/ tree (v1.1.0.0 through v1.10.0.0+), a deep scripts/ tree with per-lane tests/ (vitest), a model-benchmark scorer/ with deterministic/, grader/, lib/, and an on-disk grader cache/ (many .out.md hash files), and 5 lane-partitioned references/ + assets/ subtrees.
findings.merge_implication: The 334-file count is real and concentrated in: per-lane test suites, the model-benchmark scorer cache, and the dual verification packages (feature_catalog + manual_testing_playbook). The merge must decide whether each lane's feature_catalog category and playbook scenarios fold into a unified merged catalog or stay lane-scoped. The grader cache/ and per-lane vitest configs are runtime artifacts that move with their scripts; vitest.config.mjs at scripts/ root governs all lane tests.

findings.observation: SKILL.md §7 defines a shared termination contract: stopReason taxonomy (converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, manualStop, error, stuckRecovery) + sessionOutcome (keptBaseline, promoted, rolledBack, advisoryOnly), enforced as FROZEN helper enums in improvement-journal.cjs. Five legal-stop gate bundles (contractGate, behaviorGate, integrationGate, evidenceGate, improvementGate) must all pass for 'converged'. Lane D's own stop reasons (noTarget, promotedStop, killSwitch, visibleUnmeasurable) explicitly MAP onto this canonical taxonomy. Resume/continuation supports only 'new' lineage today (resume/restart/fork unwired).
findings.merge_implication: deep-improvement owns a frozen, journal-enforced stop-reason/outcome taxonomy that Lane D already reconciles into. This is the canonical state-machine contract the merged skill must preserve as the spine for ALL its mutation-capable modes. Other merged skills (deep-review, deep-research) likely have their own stop semantics; the merge must reconcile or namespace these without breaking the frozen-enum validator that improvement-journal.cjs enforces.

findings.area: Mutation uniqueness among the five deep loops
findings.observation: deep-improvement is explicitly THE ONLY deep loop that can mutate a file; every other deep loop (deep-research, deep-review, deep-context, deep-ai-council) is read-only or advisory. It writes only when the guarded promotion gate opens (Lane A promote-candidate.cjs canonical write; Lane D worktree promote) and always records a rollback path (rollback-candidate.cjs). It shares deep-loop-runtime for the coverage graph and atomic-state layer; system-spec-kit owns its spec folder/validation; sk-prompt-small-model owns the Lane B benchmark output tree.
findings.merge_implication: Post-merge, deep-loop-workflows would contain BOTH read-only loops and the one write-capable loop in a single skill. The merge spec must make the mutation boundary (guarded promotion + rollback) a per-mode property, ensuring read-only modes can never reach the promote/rollback helpers. The cross-skill dependencies (deep-loop-runtime backend already factored out; sk-prompt-small-model output tree; system-spec-kit spec folder) are the seams that already separate cleanly and should remain external to deep-loop-workflows.
findings.evidence: README.md:124-134 (sibling table + 'only deep loop that can mutate'); SKILL.md:289-294 (promote/rollback); SKILL.md:551-553 (integration points)

findings.observation: scripts/ holds 8 .cjs entry points + a scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs helper: convergence.cjs, upsert.cjs, query.cjs, status.cjs (the 4 ex-MCP-tool shims) and fanout-run.cjs, fanout-pool.cjs, fanout-salvage.cjs, fanout-merge.cjs (the fan-out family). SKILL.md §2 still says '4 entry points' in the resource-domain block (line 79) while §3/§6 correctly say 8 — internal drift.
findings.merge_implication: All 8 scripts + cli-guards are BACKEND and stay. Fan-out (parallel-lineage CLI orchestration) is already absorbed into the runtime — this is the precedent proving 'parallel dispatch / lineage isolation' is a backend concern, not a per-workflow concern. The merged workflows skill calls these by path; it must not re-implement fan-out per mode.

findings.observation: The runtime ALREADY absorbed council backend: lib/council/ has 7 files (multi-seat-dispatch.cjs, round-state-jsonl.cjs, adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs, cost-guards.cjs, session-state-hierarchy.cjs, PLUS council-graph-db.ts, council-graph-query.ts, convergence.cjs), and database/ has a SECOND db: council-graph.sqlite. SKILL.md §3 only enumerates the 5 .cjs council modules — it omits council-graph-db.ts, council-graph-query.ts, and lib/council/convergence.cjs.
findings.merge_implication: deep-ai-council's backend (multi-seat dispatch, round-state durability, verdict scoring, cost guards, session hierarchy, council graph DB) is ALREADY in the runtime. So merging deep-ai-council into deep-loop-workflows means moving ONLY its operator-facing/orchestration layer; its durable primitives already split out. This is the strongest single precedent for the backend/modes line. SKILL.md council enumeration needs updating to match disk.

findings.observation: deep-context ships its OWN scripts/loop-lock.cjs whose header literally states it is a 'Thin host-facing wrapper over the deep-loop-runtime loop-lock helper' — it tsx-requires deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts and exposes acquire/refresh/release as a CLI. The backend owns the lock LOGIC (loop-lock.ts); the workflow skill owns only a thin CLI adapter.
findings.merge_implication: The loop-lock CLI adapter is the kind of thin shim that should live ONCE in deep-loop-workflows (or, better, be promoted into deep-loop-runtime/scripts/ as a sibling of the other .cjs entry points so all modes share it). Today only deep-context has it; the other loops use prose-only lock steps. Merge opportunity: a single loop-lock.cjs CLI in the runtime removes the per-mode divergence.

findings.observation: reduce-state.cjs exists 4x: deep-context/, deep-research/, deep-review/, and deep-improvement/scripts/shared/. Each is the host-side state REDUCER (reads iteration JSONL/deltas, writes registry+dashboard). They carry real per-mode logic (deep-context's KIND_TO_BUCKET, agreement-weighting, DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_GATE/AGREEMENT_MIN that explicitly 'mirror' coverage-graph-signals constants) but also re-implement shared mechanics (writeTextAtomic that 'mirrors the runtime writeStateAtomic contract').
findings.merge_implication: The reducers are MODE logic and move into deep-loop-workflows, but their SHARED mechanics (atomic text write, the relevance/agreement constants currently DUPLICATED from coverage-graph-signals) are backend and should be imported, not mirrored. Merge risk: 4 reducers drifting on constants that are supposed to mirror one backend source. Consolidate the shared atomic-write + gate constants into the runtime; keep only the per-mode bucket/rollup logic in workflows.

findings.observation: runtime-capabilities.cjs is duplicated in deep-research/ and deep-review/scripts/. deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs is a generic mode-switching loop orchestrator (routes agent-improvement / model-benchmark / skill-benchmark / non-dev-ai-system-refine) whose own header notes 'deep-improvement has no loop.cjs'. deep-improvement also carries a large scripts/shared/ (parse-args, loop-host, reduce-state, promote-candidate, model-family, etc.) plus scripts/lib/ (typed-errors, profile-resolve, promotion-gates, mirror-sync-verify).
findings.merge_implication: runtime-capabilities (probing what the runtime supports) is arguably backend — one copy belongs in deep-loop-runtime, not duplicated per workflow. loop-host is a mode-dispatcher that is exactly the 'modes/lanes' layer deep-loop-workflows is meant to own — it stays in workflows but becomes the canonical multi-mode entry seam. deep-improvement's scripts/shared/ is the richest per-skill toolbox and the hardest merge: separate its generic plumbing (parse-args, typed-errors) from its improvement-specific lanes.

Production imports still reach into system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules for zod + better-sqlite3 (why graph-metadata depends_on system-spec-kit).
findings.merge_implication: Tests + references stay BACKEND. The merge MUST preserve the vitest glob path and the runtime's dependency on system-spec-kit node_modules — moving/renaming deep-loop-runtime would break test discovery and the zod/better-sqlite3 import paths. integration_points.md §7.1 + §Note also reveal a hidden coupling: a mcp_server test imports deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs, so the reducer's effective test boundary already crosses skills — relevant when reduce-state moves into deep-loop-workflows.

readers.slice: runtime consumption — how each of the five deep-* skills consumes deep-loop-runtime entry points

findings.observation: Two distinct consumption modes exist. (1) Indirect-via-command-YAML: deep-context, deep-research, deep-review consume runtime CLI scripts NOT from their own SKILL.md/scripts but from the command-layer loop YAMLs (`.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-*-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`), which invoke `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/{fanout-run,fanout-merge,convergence,upsert}.cjs --loop-type <context|research|review>`. (2) Direct shell-out from skill script: deep-ai-council's own `scripts/replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs` resolves the repo root and shells out to `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --loop-type council` (replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs:26,56,65); its command YAML (deep_ask-ai-council_*.yaml:50,52) also wires `upsert.cjs --loop-type council` and `convergence.cjs --loop-type council`.
findings.merge_implication: The runtime call sites are mostly in the COMMAND layer, not inside the skill bodies being merged. Merging the five skills into deep-loop-workflows does NOT by itself move these invocations — the per-mode loop YAMLs under `commands/deep/assets/` remain the real coupling surface and must be inventoried separately. The merged skill's contract should document that runtime entry points are dispatched by the command YAML, parameterized by `--loop-type`, which is already the natural per-mode dimension a single merged skill would carry.

findings.area: Shared runtime CLI surface (4 of 5 skills are uniform)
findings.observation: context/research/review/council all consume the SAME four runtime CLI scripts with only the `--loop-type` value differing: `fanout-run.cjs` (CLI lineage pool driver), `fanout-merge.cjs` (per-mode merge: dedup for research, strongest-restriction for review, agreement for context), `convergence.cjs` (CONTINUE/STOP_ALLOWED/STOP_BLOCKED), `upsert.cjs` (coverage-graph node/edge writer). The runtime also exposes `query.cjs`, `status.cjs`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`. Loop-type vocabulary is centrally validated in `deep-loop-runtime/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` (`VALID_KINDS[loopType]`).
